payload.summary: Accept the amendment. The current journal dedupe key collapses distinct named bead starts that legitimately share the same bead, project, and timestamp, so a real retry start can disappear from the audit trail.

votes.rationale: `record_bead_start` writes `BeadStarted` with details only `project={project_id}`, and `append_event_if_missing` deduplicates on `(timestamp, event_type, bead_id, details)`. The lineage/model logic now allows a distinct named run to start even when an earlier finalized run for the same bead/project shares that `started_at`, so two valid starts can become journal-identical and the later one is silently dropped. That is a real correctness issue because the journal no longer faithfully records milestone history and diverges from the lineage store, which distinguishes attempts by `run_id` and `plan_hash`. Including run identity in `BeadStarted` details, or otherwise widening dedupe for start events while preserving idempotent same-attempt retries, is the right fix.
